title: LittlePrince_MEG_French_Listen_Pallier2025
description: This dataset comprises magnetoencephalography (MEG) recordings from 58 healthy French-speaking adults listening to the French audiobook of *Le Petit Prince*. Participants completed 9 runs of continuous listening while MEG data were acquired at 1000 Hz using a whole-head Elekta Neuromag TRIUX system, with concurrent electrooculography and electrocardiography. High-resolution T1-weighted anatomical MRI scans were acquired for each participant to enable source localization and cortical surface reconstruction.
methods_description: MEG data were recorded continuously at 1000 Hz using a whole-head Elekta Neuromag TRIUX system (102 magnetometers, 204 planar gradiometers) with online filtering (0.1–330 Hz). Auditory stimuli were delivered through MEG-compatible earphones at individually adjusted comfortable listening levels. Vertical and horizontal electrooculograms and electrocardiograms were recorded simultaneously using bipolar electrodes. High-resolution T1-weighted anatomical MRI scans were acquired on a 3T Siemens Magnetom Prisma scanner using a standard MPRAGE sequence for coregistration and source analysis.
